request.message: put CeraVe and La Roche-Posay moisturizers side by side in a table

response_body.response: don't see specific product comparisons in your current docs. here's what i can pull together on both brands based on what's in skinmax: both CeraVe and La Roche-Posay show up as moisturizer options that fit the absorption ladder. the protocol calls for a moisturizer with ceramides + lipids to lock in hydration and repair barrier. here's a side-by-side on what each brand typically delivers:  if your skin runs dry or damaged, CeraVe's ceramide-heavy formulas fit the protocol better. if you're oily or reactive, La Roche-Posay's lighter texture is the move. either works as long as it has ceramides and locks hydration in.

products.name: CeraVe PM Facial Moisturizing Lotion
products.description: Lightweight ceramide + niacinamide moisturizer for nightly use.

options.name: CeraVe
cons: can feel occlusive for oily skin
